editionData.title: July 14, 2026: Trump says U.S. military will reimpose blockade on Iranian ports; U.K. to designate Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a...
editionData.description: Global headlines: Trump says U.S. military will reimpose blockade on Iranian ports (The president said the U.S. will impose a 20 percent fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, contradicting official assertions that fees would violate international law); U.K. to designate Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist group (U.K. authorities have linked the IRGC and its proxies to a wave of violent plots on British soil); and Vatican hits back at U.S. claim that Pope Leo speaks as a politician (President Donald Trump's ambassador had sought to portray the pontiff as the political leader of the Holy See. The Vatican swiftly said he is “proclaiming the Gospel.”). Markets leaned lower, with 6 of 7 tracked indexes down; NASDAQ Composite down 1.55% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: Japan develops a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries (In a real win for Earth Day, it has been reported that scientists in Japan have developed a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from...); Fundamentals of Wireless Communication (web.stanford.edu: Fundamentals of Wireless Communication); and The Git history command deserves more attention (Working with lots of changes in parallel on git can be painful. You end up juggling branches and commits, and running scary rebase -i...). Product Hunt features Loomal (Monetize any MCP server in 5 minutes with no % skim), Fudge MCP (Give your AI agents design taste from existing websites), and AgentKey (One-stop live data marketplace for your agent).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: Trump says U.S. military will reimpose blockade on Iranian ports
headlines.summary: The president said the U.S. will impose a 20 percent fee on shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, contradicting official assertions that fees would violate international law.

headlines.title: U.K. to designate Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps as a terrorist group
headlines.summary: U.K. authorities have linked the IRGC and its proxies to a wave of violent plots on British soil.

headlines.title: Vatican hits back at U.S. claim that Pope Leo speaks as a politician
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump's ambassador had sought to portray the pontiff as the political leader of the Holy See. The Vatican swiftly said he is “proclaiming the Gospel.”

headlines.title: Fire at Bangkok bar kills 27; exits may have been blocked, officials say
headlines.summary: Preliminary investigations indicated that the fire may have been started by electrical short circuits, and that emergency exits may have been blocked.

headlines.title: Renovated Buckingham Palace will be a very royal office building
headlines.summary: Since Queen Victoria made it her official residence in 1837, every British sovereign has lived there. King Charles III, born at the palace, is the first to opt out.

headlines.title: Mexico’s Sheinbaum announces criminal charges request in US over ICE deaths
headlines.summary: Mexico to formally ask prosecutors to file cases in US courts over its citizens' deaths in immigration operations.

headlines.title: Brazil court bars Bolsonaro’s son from visiting ex-president for 90 days
headlines.summary: Brazil’s top court bars Flavio Bolsonaro from visiting his father for 90 days over a social media post.

headlines.title: Hungary’s parliament votes to oust president in latest anti-Orban move
headlines.summary: Hungarian parliament passes amendment that would remove President Sulyok, appointed under ex-Prime Minister Orban.

headlines.title: Record El Niño threatens to unleash floods across East Africa and Asia
headlines.summary: Aid groups warn a record-strength El Niño could unleash flooding and hunger from Somalia to Pakistan.

headlines.title: How Kenyan volunteers hunt polio’s hidden trail
headlines.summary: Community health volunteers travel across remote northern Kenya to detect poliovirus before it can spread.

headlines.title: Trump's HHS abandons threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care
headlines.summary: NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.

headlines.title: South Carolina's governor names Lindsey Graham's sister to serve out his term
headlines.summary: South Carolina's governor says he is appointing Darline Graham to finish the term of her late brother. Top Republicans, including President Trump, have coalesced around the pick.

headlines.title: On foreign policy, Graham's influence may have been felt the most
headlines.summary: Over the course of his three decades in Congress, Lindsey Graham was a major influence on Capitol Hill and throughout the world as one of the most vocal advocates for U.S. military might.

headlines.title: The U.S. strikes Iran after Trump announces a renewed blockade and tolls in Hormuz
headlines.summary: The U.S. and Iran are fighting for control of the Strait of Hormuz, threatening a return to all-out war after agreeing to a ceasefire last month.

headlines.title: States sue to stop Paramount-Warner Bros blockbuster merger
headlines.summary: California is among the states suing to block Paramount from buying Warner Bros. Discovery in a Hollywood mega-merger that would unite some of the nation's largest movie studios and TV newsrooms.

headlines.title: Iowa hosts Shanghai youth for softball and cultural exchange
headlines.summary: Yellow soybean seeds may have first connected Iowa and China, but today, yellow softballs and student exchanges are carrying that relationship forward.

headlines.title: Man killed during ICE encounter in Maine
headlines.summary: A man was killed by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent early Monday morning in a coastal city in Maine, according to officials.

headlines.title: Iran, US vie for Hormuz control amid more strikes
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran each asserted on Monday that they controlled the Strait of Hormuz, after a weekend of attacks marked the sharpest escalation in violence since an interim peace deal was reached last month, further threatening any diplomatic efforts to end the protracted war in the Middle East.

headlines.title: Tech experts, economists warn of rapid AI disruption
headlines.summary: More than 200 technology experts and economists have signed a statement warning of the sudden transformation that AI may cause in the economy.

headlines.title: Lula's Mercosur-China trade deal idea welcomed
headlines.summary: Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva's call for the Southern Common Market, or Mercosur, to pursue a free trade agreement with China has been welcomed by experts as an encouraging signal of closer engagement with Beijing.

headlines.title: Autonomous hill councils to be extended to all seven districts in Ladakh
headlines.summary: Ladakh announces Autonomous Hill Development Councils for all seven districts, enhancing grassroots governance and democratic decentralisation.

headlines.title: Kuki-Zo Council meets IB chief, seeks govt. action on blockade by Nagas
headlines.summary: Kuki-Zo Council urges government intervention against Naga blockades impacting essential supplies in Manipur amid ongoing ethnic tensions.

headlines.title: At Jantar Mantar, fasting student hospitalised while Wangchuk loses 8 kg in CJP hunger strike
headlines.summary: Wangchuk's health deteriorates on Day 24 of CJP protest, with growing support from AAP and CPI(M) leaders amid urgent demands.

headlines.title: Border States flag illegal migrants on voter rolls, use of drones, apps by smugglers
headlines.summary: Border states address illegal migrants on voter rolls, drone smuggling, and infrastructure issues at historic police chiefs' conference.

headlines.title: The political storm in Punjab surrounding ‘Satluj’ movie | Explained
headlines.summary: Explore the political controversy in Punjab surrounding the film 'Satluj' and its implications for historical memory and justice.

headlines.title: Nvidia halves Asia AI chip customer list, FT reports
headlines.summary: Reuters: Nvidia halves Asia AI chip customer list, FT reports.

headlines.title: Inside South Korea's world-beating bear market
headlines.summary: Reuters: Inside South Korea's world-beating bear market.

headlines.title: Trump slashes the size of two Utah national monuments
headlines.summary: Reuters: Trump slashes the size of two Utah national monuments.

headlines.title: Trump sends Congress formal notice that Iran conflict has resumed
headlines.summary: Reuters: Trump sends Congress formal notice that Iran conflict has resumed.

headlines.title: UAE says Iranian missiles struck oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz, one sailor killed
headlines.summary: Reuters: UAE says Iranian missiles struck oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz, one sailor killed.

hackerNews.title: Japan develops a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries
hackerNews.summary: In a real win for Earth Day, it has been reported that scientists in Japan have developed a method to recover up to 90% of lithium from used EV batteries

hackerNews.title: The Git history command deserves more attention
hackerNews.summary: Working with lots of changes in parallel on git can be painful. You end up juggling branches and commits, and running scary rebase -i commands that can leave your tree in a half-broken state if you so much as sneeze. jj, an alternative to git, gets discussed a lot these days (1, 2, 3, 4) and is often pitched as a solution. While I’m very sold on the problems jj is trying to solve, the way it solves them hasn’t quite hit home with me. Every 3 months, for the last 1.5 years, I try it out for a few days, really trying to make it part of my workflow but eventually I give up and go back to git.1 That’s where git history comes in. It’s an experimental command that arrived across two releases, 2.54 (April, reword and split subcommands) and 2.55 (June, fixup subcommand). It got a flurry of attention on each release day, and then, as far as I can tell, not much community discussion since. Which is a shame, because IMO it already delivers several of the benefits people tout for jj without needing to switch your whole workflow. And the cool thing is that it’s part of the core git distribution, so you can try it without installing anything.
